task_completion.description: Log in to the Splunk Enterprise web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Enterprise web interface is open in a browser at localhost:8000, and the dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login (likely as 'admin').

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Add Data or Data Inputs section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Data' workspace or 'Data Inputs' settings page is visible in the Splunk UI.

task_completion.description: Configure a monitor for the specified file path
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Add Data' wizard (Monitor option), the file path '/var/log/kern.log' is entered into the File or Directory input field.
In the 'Set Source Type' step of the wizard, the sourcetype dropdown or input is set to 'syslog'.
In the 'Input Settings' step of the wizard, the Index dropdown or input is set to 'system_logs'.
A 'Review' screen showing the correct path, sourcetype, and index, OR a success message indicating the file input has been created successfully.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk web interface instead of CLI
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions are performed within a web browser navigating the Splunk GUI, rather than using terminal commands like 'splunk add monitor'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk Add Data wizard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the progression through the distinct steps of the Splunk Add Data wizard (Select Source, Set Source Type, Input Settings, Review).

integrity.description: Agent did not spoof the result in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and success messages appear within the genuine Splunk web application interface, not typed out in a text editor or terminal echo.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must use the Splunk web interface at http://localhost:8000. The credentials are username 'admin' and password 'SplunkAdmin1!'. The exact file path to monitor is '/var/log/kern.log'. The sourcetype must be set to 'syslog'. The target index must be exactly 'system_logs'.
